inclusion_criteria: Study design: randomized or nonrandomized controlled trials and prospective cohort studies that compare groups by sodium intake level.
Population: adults (≥18 years) from the general population (community-dwelling), not restricted to a specific disease cohort.

Exposure/comparator: includes at least one group meeting recommended low sodium intake (≤5 g/day salt or ≤2 g/day sodium) compared with higher/usual intake, with sodium intake quantified by validated methods (e.g., 24-h urinary sodium, calibrated dietary assessment).

Outcomes: reports at least one of the following—blood pressure, cardiovascular disease events, physical performance, composite renal outcomes or chronic kidney disease, osteoporosis (e.g., bone mineral density or fractures), or all-cause mortality.
exclusion_criteria: Wrong population: studies exclusively in children/adolescents, pregnant individuals, or highly specific patient cohorts (e.g., heart failure, dialysis, transplant) not representative of the general adult population.
Wrong design: cross-sectional, case-control, case series, case reports, ecological or modeling studies, reviews, editorials, or protocols without results.

Wrong exposure/comparator: sodium intake not quantified or no group at/under the recommended low-sodium threshold; interventions not primarily dietary sodium (e.g., sodium-containing medications or infusions).
Wrong outcomes: does not report any of the specified outcomes.
screening_criteria.description: Screening criteria to identify studies in general adult populations that compare low (≤5 g/day salt or ≤2 g/day sodium) versus higher sodium intake and report prespecified health outcomes.
